name: azure-web-pubsub-ts
description: Build real-time messaging applications using Azure Web PubSub SDKs for JavaScript (@azure/web-pubsub, @azure/web-pubsub-client). Use when implementing WebSocket-based real-time features, pub/sub messaging, group chat, or live notifications.
capabilities: Generate client access tokens with user authentication and custom roles
Send broadcast, user-targeted, and group-specific messages
Manage connection groups and handle WebSocket events
Implement Express middleware for real-time event handlers
Authenticate clients using Azure Entra ID or access keys
Handle connection lifecycle events (connected, disconnected, rejoin)
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains legitimate Azure Web PubSub SDK documentation. Static analyzer produced 35 false positive external_commands findings by misinterpreting markdown code block backticks as shell execution. Network and environment variable patterns are standard Azure SDK usage. No malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: Implement WebSocket-based real-time messaging in your applications using Azure Web PubSub SDKs. This skill provides production-ready patterns for pub/sub messaging, group management, and connection handling with TypeScript.
userTitle: Build Real-Time Apps with Azure Web PubSub
limitations: Requires Azure Web PubSub resource and valid connection string or endpoint
Client-side code requires server-side negotiate endpoint implementation
Express handler requires Express.js framework integration
Does not cover advanced scaling or multi-region deployment patterns

useCases.title: Real-Time Chat Application
useCases.description: Build a multi-room chat system with user authentication, group messaging, and presence indicators using Azure Web PubSub groups and user-targeted messaging.

useCases.targetUser: Backend engineers implementing real-time data streaming
useCases.title: Live Dashboard Updates
useCases.description: Push real-time data updates to connected dashboards using broadcast messaging. Ideal for monitoring systems, analytics displays, or live metrics visualization.

useCases.targetUser: Game developers adding real-time multiplayer features
useCases.title: Multiplayer Game Lobby
useCases.description: Create game rooms with player matchmaking, in-game messaging, and session management using groups and connection management APIs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up Azure Web PubSub server-side client using the connection string from environment variables. Create a hub named 'chat' and generate a basic client access token.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with Azure Web PubSub integration

promptTemplates.title: Authenticated Token Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a client access token for user 'user123' with roles for joining groups, sending to groups, and auto-join the 'chat-room' group. Set expiration to 60 minutes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Implementing user authentication with custom permissions

promptTemplates.title: Client-Side Connection
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a WebPubSubClient that connects via a negotiate endpoint. Register handlers for connected, group-message, and disconnected events. Join the 'chat-room' group after connection.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a real-time client with event handling

promptTemplates.title: Express Event Handler
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement an Express middleware handler for the 'chat' hub with handleConnect validation, handleUserEvent processing, and onConnected logging. Include a negotiate endpoint that generates tokens for authenticated users.
promptTemplates.scenario: Production-ready server with authentication and event handling

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production instead of access keys for better security
Register all event handlers before calling client.start() to avoid missing initial events
Validate authentication in handleConnect to reject unauthorized connections early in the handshake
antiPatterns: Hardcoding connection strings or access keys directly in source code instead of using environment variables
Accessing process.env variables without TypeScript non-null assertion or fallback values
Sending messages before verifying client connection state is 'connected'

faq.question: What is the difference between @azure/web-pubsub and @azure/web-pubsub-client?
faq.answer: @azure/web-pubsub is the server-side SDK for managing hubs, generating tokens, and sending messages. @azure/web-pubsub-client is the browser/Node.js client SDK for connecting to WebSocket endpoints and handling real-time messages.

faq.question: How do I authenticate users with Azure Web PubSub?
faq.answer: Use either Azure Entra ID with DefaultAzureCredential (recommended for production) or generate tokens with userId and roles using the server SDK. Clients connect using the generated token URL.

faq.question: What happens when a client disconnects unexpectedly?
faq.answer: Azure Web PubSub automatically attempts reconnection. Handle the 'disconnected' event for cleanup and 'rejoin-group-failed' to handle cases where group rejoin fails after reconnection.

faq.question: Can I send messages to specific users versus all users?
faq.answer: Yes. Use sendToAll() for broadcast, sendToUser() for user-targeted messages (delivers to all their connections), or sendToConnection() for a specific connection ID.

faq.question: How do groups work in Azure Web PubSub?
faq.answer: Groups are logical channels within a hub. Users can join/leave groups dynamically. Messages sent to a group reach all connections in that group. Useful for chat rooms, game lobbies, or topic-based subscriptions.

faq.question: What is the negotiate endpoint pattern?
faq.answer: The negotiate endpoint is a server route that generates client access tokens. Clients call this endpoint to get a WebSocket URL with access token, then connect using that URL. This keeps credentials server-side.
